page: Amazon Web Services c6in.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores using the x86_64 architecture, specifically utilizing Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and EBS optimization.

A key differentiator of the c6in.32xlarge is its high-speed baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score in the top 25% and a Passmark CPU score in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 332.31 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 131.82 GB/sec. The processor's support for advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI, contributes to top-tier performance in Passmark CPU extended instructions and encryption tests.

Given its compute-heavy profile and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, the c6in.32xlarge is designed for network-intensive and compute-bound workloads. It is highly suitable for tasks such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 69.41 GB/sec, as well as database operations and software compilation. The instance also supports moderate machine learning tasks, delivering strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLM models. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the lack of bundled local storage and GPUs positions this instance as a specialized, high-density compute resource where users pay primarily for raw CPU cycles and high-throughput networking.
description: Amazon Web Services c6in.32xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but offers a high baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in Passmark CPU and encryption tests, alongside strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. It is highly efficient for network-bound and compute-intensive workloads, such as high-throughput web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and encryption performance.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring 128 vCPUs, 200 Gbps network bandwidth, and advanced cryptographic instruction support.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processors.
256.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 2.0 GB per core ratio.
High-speed baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps for network-intensive tasks.
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU, encryption, and ray tracing benchmarks.
Optimized for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
